description_long: This bungalow features a steep blue gable roof with two prominent dormer windows on the front, flanking a central entrance beneath a small gabled porch. The facade is symmetrical, with evenly spaced rectangular windows on either side of the main door, all illuminated with a warm yellow glow. The structure is supported by visible wooden posts along the front porch, and the walls are finished in a light beige color with darker trim. Both sides of the building display three arched windows, while the rear is plain with no visible openings. The roof overhangs the walls, providing additional shelter and architectural interest. The overall design suggests a cozy, traditional residential dwelling suitable for a suburban or neighborhood setting.
description: A symmetrical bungalow with a steep blue gable roof, dormer windows, and a central porch entrance, featuring warm-lit windows and wooden trim.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, two dormer windows, symmetrical window arrangement, and the covered porch supported by posts.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the overhanging roof, three arched side windows, and the profile of the dormers.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a plain wall with no windows or doors, emphasizing the full span of the blue gable roof.
description_view_3: Right side view mirroring the left, with three arched windows and the roof's overhang and dormer profiles.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical front-facing windows with grouped arched side windows
